title: centaurea maculosa subsp. biebersteinii
intro: Centaurea maculosa subsp. biebersteinii, also known as spotted knapweed or spotted knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Centaurea maculosa subsp. biebersteinii include its ability to relieve congestion and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant are commonly consumed as teas, infusions, or dried extracts, and are also used topically in the form of creams and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
